title: Bridge Essay: The empirical status of predictive coding and active inference.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent-arising** (Phase 6) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Dependent-arising to mechanistic neuroscience.

**The empirical status of predictive coding and active inference.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev. PMID: 38030100. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2023.105473. Research on predictive processing models has focused largely on two specific algorithmic theories: Predictive Coding for perception and Active Inference for decision-making. While these interconnected theories possess broad explanatory potential, they have only recently begun to receive direct empirical evaluation. Here, we review recent studies of Predictive Coding and Active Inference with afocus on evaluating the degree to which they are empirically supported. For Predictive Coding, we find
